stimulus: Wangari Maathai was a Kenyan environmental and political activist who founded the Green Belt Movement in Nairobi in 1977. She was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in ______blank through her decades of advocacy, communities across rural Kenya had planted tens of millions of trees.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is the coordination of main clauses within a sentence. This choice correctly uses a comma and the coordinating conjunction “and” to join the first main clause (“She...2004”) and the second main clause (“through...trees”).

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence. Choice B is incorrect because when coordinating two longer main clauses, it is conventional to use a comma before the coordinating conjunction. Choice D is incorrect because, without a conjunction following it, a comma can’t join two main clauses (comma splice).
